mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE rises by over 0.5 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points in the same period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers immediate market repricing of future central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.6 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates signals strong labor market absorption, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence yields negative 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of observed periods where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55 percent of cases over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production indicates immediate supply chain bottlenecks or demand destruction, forcing rapid equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of months where INDPRO drops by over 1.0 percent month-over-month.
